commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh The strong among the mighty shall speak to him out of the midst of sheol with them that help him: they are gone down, they lie uncircumcised, slain by the sword. Septuagint, "giants shall say to thee: Remain in the depth of the pit. Whom dost thou excel. descend "(Haydock) They vary much in this chapter from the Hebrew. Jerome) Helpers; Lybians, chap. These shall come to compliment the king. (Calmet) After a battle, those of the same nation were buried together, ver. (Theodoret) The Egyptians had tombs like houses, in which there were separate holes or apartments (Calmet) as in large vaults.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
